hypotheses.summary: Couple a tiny anchor4 front-z precursor to the already-winning anchor5 far-z seed mainline, so the contiguous 4→5 front corridor is preconditioned one anchor earlier without touching the late relay core. This is structurally different from both the isolated anchor4 front-z basin and the isolated anchor5 far-z basin.

Insert a tiny closed x-family pair at anchor2, the earliest repeated x-boundary anchor before the anchor3 inner handoff, then keep the full anchor5 mainline untouched. This probes an early x-boundary observability gate that is outside the tested z-corridor / relay neighborhoods.

Open a mixed-beta diagonal butterfly in the anchor3→4 handoff region before any front-z outer rotation. Listed as a plausible early-gateway family, but not spent in this reset-window batch because the chain and boundary-seed variants are lower-risk first probes.

Split a tiny boundary-control pair across anchors2 and3 as a bookend conditioner before the front corridor. Left listed only because it is a weaker coupled version of H2 and would consume batch budget without isolated evidence.

tested_hypotheses.summary: Couple a tiny anchor4 front-z precursor to the already-winning anchor5 far-z seed mainline, so the contiguous 4→5 front corridor is preconditioned one anchor earlier without touching the late relay core. This is structurally different from both the isolated anchor4 front-z basin and the isolated anchor5 far-z basin.

Insert a tiny closed x-family pair at anchor2, the earliest repeated x-boundary anchor before the anchor3 inner handoff, then keep the full anchor5 mainline untouched. This probes an early x-boundary observability gate that is outside the tested z-corridor / relay neighborhoods.

candidate_specs.name: chainfront_l4_neg1_l5_neg6_plus_relaymax_unified_l9y2p5
candidate_specs.rationale: Coupled front-corridor chain: add a tiny anchor4 precursor in front of the current anchor5 mainline, so the 4→5 front-half z corridor is preconditioned before the untouched late relay core.

candidate_specs.name: chainfront_l4_neg2_l5_neg6_plus_relaymax_unified_l9y2p5
candidate_specs.rationale: Slightly stronger coupled front-corridor chain with a 2 s anchor4 precursor in front of the same anchor5 mainline.

candidate_specs.name: xboundary_l2_neg2_plus_zseed_l5_neg6_plus_relaymax_unified_l9y2p5
candidate_specs.rationale: Early x-boundary seed: add a short negative x-family closed pair at anchor2, then keep the full anchor5 mainline untouched.

candidate_specs.name: xboundary_l2_pos2_plus_zseed_l5_neg6_plus_relaymax_unified_l9y2p5
candidate_specs.rationale: Sign-control companion for the early x-boundary seed: flip the anchor2 x-family excursion to the positive sweep direction while holding the rest of the mainline fixed.

rows_sorted.rationale: Sign-control companion for the early x-boundary seed: flip the anchor2 x-family excursion to the positive sweep direction while holding the rest of the mainline fixed.
Coupled front-corridor chain: add a tiny anchor4 precursor in front of the current anchor5 mainline, so the 4→5 front-half z corridor is preconditioned before the untouched late relay core.
Slightly stronger coupled front-corridor chain with a 2 s anchor4 precursor in front of the same anchor5 mainline.
Early x-boundary seed: add a short negative x-family closed pair at anchor2, then keep the full anchor5 mainline untouched.

kf36_recheck.reason: Not triggered because the best landing xboundary_l2_pos2_plus_zseed_l5_neg6_plus_relaymax_unified_l9y2p5 = 10.523 / 0.983 / 99.574 still sits too far from both `zseed_l5_neg6_plus_relaymax_unified_l9y2p5` and `entryrelay_l8x1_l9y1_unifiedcore` to count as genuinely competitive.

bottom_line.scientific_conclusion: This batch did test two genuinely new post-frontz structures beyond the already-pruned basins: a distributed anchor4→5 front-corridor chain and an early anchor2 x-boundary seed family. The best landed point was `xboundary_l2_pos2_plus_zseed_l5_neg6_plus_relaymax_unified_l9y2p5` = 10.523 / 0.983 / 99.574. Relative to `zseed_l5_neg6_plus_relaymax_unified_l9y2p5` it moved by Δmean -1.180, Δmedian -0.290, and Δmax +0.002; relative to `entryrelay_l8x1_l9y1_unifiedcore` it moved by Δmean -0.909, Δmedian +0.187, and Δmax +0.000. The only nontrivial signal was that the positive-sign anchor2 x-boundary seed produced a razor-thin max-only edge, but it paid far too much mean to count as a stronger usable direction. So this post-frontz hidden-family batch did not uncover a stronger direction.
